statement.p: In the following, all vectors are in {\mathbb R}^n. An orthogonal set is a set of vectors all of which are orthogonal to the other vectors in the set (but not necessarily orthonormal).

li.p: The orthogonal projection of y onto v is the same as the orthogonal projection of y onto cv whenever c\neq 0.
If the columns of an m\times n matrix are orthonormal, then the linear mapping x\rightarrow Ax preserves lengths.
If a set S= \{ u_{1},...,u_{p} \} has the property that u_{i}\cdot u_{j}=0 whenever i\neq j, then S is an orthonormal set.
Not every orthogonal set in {\mathbb R}^n is a linearly independent set.
